posts.author: niftymonk
posts.text: MTF books are growing fast. What could go wrong? — Leverage works brilliantly until liquidity disappears. Comparing mortgages and education loans to concentrated MTF exposure in small/mid-cap stocks is a category error. A house doesn’t hit lower circuits for 5 straight sessions. An MTF collateral basket can. Also, saying ‘the government will take care of default risk’ is exactly the mindset that creates financial blowups. Private profits with socialized losses are not a risk framework; it’s a moral hazard. Nobody is arguing against credit expansion. The point is simple: leveraged speculation c…

posts.author: Rockytrader
posts.text: MTF books are growing fast. What could go wrong? — how are brokers actually managing the risk on the exploding MTF book (₹1.22 lakh crore now)? If brokers are borrowing money from banks/NBFCs to fund client MTF leverage and essentially betting on the same stocks their clients are buying aren’t they taking on massive directional risk? In a sharp correction, especially in illiquid mid/small-cap names that hit circuit breakers, clients can’t exit, collateral value crashes, and the broker ends up holding the bag. Couldn’t this easily wipe out a broker’s entire net worth and even push some into ban…

posts.author: RupeeRiser
posts.text: MTF books are growing fast. What could go wrong? — If this country needs to grow further , it needs to start leveraging even more on its exiting assets even at an individual levels. Let the Regulator and Government take care of the Default risk (in case , if needed). The Financial Institutions that are licensed to participate in the country’s economic infra-structure should make it easy for individuals to access credit or leverage existing assets ( investments ) to bring more monetary activity into the domestic economy. Look for example USA and the Europe, everything in leveraged to the max ev…

posts.author: nithin_kumrr
posts.text: MTF books are growing fast. What could go wrong? — MTF trades are treated like delivery trades. So, if you’re investing and selling within 1 year, it is considered STCG. Mentioned here .

posts.author: Shubhanshu
posts.text: MTF books are growing fast. What could go wrong? — MTF books across brokers have grown sharply over the last few years, even though the broader market hasn’t really gone anywhere. Quoting @nithin ’s tweet: MTF books are growing across brokers despite the broader markets going nowhere. This isn’t like the Korean markets, for example, where the markets are up 150% in the last year alone, and people are borrowing to ride that rally. Our situation is different. The big risk with MTF is the risk of the stock becoming illiquid in case there’s a sharp market fall. If a stock moves more than the margi…

posts.author: Adarsh_Patil
posts.text: Zerodha MTF facility on intrday trades — If you trade intraday, statutory charges are applied based on the intraday slab (0.025% on the sell side), not based on the product type you use.

posts.text: Zerodha MTF facility on intrday trades — Hi For intraday MTF trades, would the STT be 0.1% on both buy and sell sides. Or considered an intraday trade with 0.025% on sell sides only?
